title: Showy Flaky Norwegian Rye Flatbread
description: Earthy, flaky flatbreads made with rye and whole wheat flour, finished with caraway, dill, and a touch of honey for a showoff Norwegian lunch treat.
instructions: Mix rye flour, whole wheat flour, dry yeast, salt, and caraway seeds in a large bowl.
Add warm water, honey, and olive oil; stir until a shaggy dough forms.
Transfer dough to a lightly floured surface and knead for 6–8 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm spot until doubled in volume (≈30–35 minutes).
Punch down the dough, divide it into eight equal portions, and shape each into a round disc about 1/4 inch thick using a rolling pin.
Heat a non‑stick skillet or griddle over medium‑high heat; no oil is needed as the dough is already hydrated.
Cook the first flatbread for 2–3 minutes until golden brown and puffed, then flip and cover loosely with a lid to steam for 30 seconds.
Repeat for each disc, keeping the cooked flatbreads warm in a low‑heat oven or covered blanket.
While cooking, lightly sprinkle the tops with fresh dill and lemon zest for a fresh, aromatic finish.
Serve the flatbreads warm with a side of smoked salmon, cucumber salad, or a light yogurt dip.
